reasoning: Achieved occupancy is only 2.1%, which is critically low and far below the 35% threshold. The root cause is the combination of a tiny block size (32 threads = 1 warp) with an enormous dynamic shared memory allocation of 64 KB per block (16384 floats × 4 bytes), which severely limits the number of blocks that can co-reside on an SM. With DRAM utilization at only 0.3% and long scoreboard stalls at just 3.4%, the GPU is neither memory-bound nor latency-bound in the traditional sense — it simply has too few active warps to keep any functional units busy.
raw_response:
BOTTLENECK: occupancy-limited
CONFIDENCE: HIGH
REASONING: Achieved occupancy is only 2.1%, which is critically low and far below the 35% threshold. The root cause is the combination of a tiny block size (32 threads = 1 warp) with an enormous dynamic shared memory allocation of 64 KB per block (16384 floats × 4 bytes), which severely limits the number of blocks that can co-reside on an SM. With DRAM utilization at only 0.3% and long scoreboard stalls at just 3.4%, the GPU is neither memory-bound nor latency-bound in the traditional sense — it simply has too few active warps to keep any functional units busy.

prompt.system: You are a GPU performance engineer. Follow the requested format exactly.

judge.explanation: The response correctly identifies occupancy-limited as the bottleneck with high confidence, cites specific metric values (2.1% occupancy, 0.3% DRAM utilization, 3.4% scoreboard stalls, 64KB shared memory, 32-thread block size), and correctly attributes the root cause to launch geometry and shared memory constraints rather than the misleading compute-heavy signal.
